source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools. We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), and Gokul Rajaram. We've 3x'd our ARR this year, and our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

the action layer for AI agents is the least-solved part of the stack. this role works at that frontier: embedded with the customers pushing hardest, turning open research questions into tools their agents use, shipping the code yourself.

THE FRONTIER YOU'LL WORK ON

• turning an 800-endpoint enterprise API into a tool surface a model can navigate. tool granularity, composite tools, description engineering, scope design. no textbook exists; you write it in production.

• building the evals that tell us an agent picked the right tool. the field has no good answer; your harnesses become ours.

• working directly with the engineers defining what agents can do, and feeding what breaks back into the product.

WHAT YOU'LL DO?

• own enterprise accounts end to end: discovery, spec, implementation, QA, release.

• ship production code in our toolkit monorepo and customer SDKs: custom and composite tools, schema and scope changes, per-customer patches.

• turn large enterprise APIs into agent-usable tool surfaces, and build the evals that prove the model picks the right tool.

• QA toolkits, and build the harnesses that make QA repeatable instead of artisanal.

• run weekly customer syncs: the summaries, ETAs, and escalations that keep an engagement coherent.

• operate per-customer tool-patching as self-service, not escalation.

• dogfood Composio on your own workflows.

"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

• applied integration engineering

– you ship production code, not demos.

– you live in enterprise APIs and auth: OAuth 2.0, consent models, scope design, multi workspace tokens. you know the edge cases from scars.

– you make architecture calls under pressure and defend them to a customer's principal engineers.

• customer technical ownership

– you've taken a named enterprise account from first call to live.– you turn vague asks into specs, and push back when the ask is wrong.

• ai native

– you have built with the language models.

– you have built for the language models, and treat the agent-tool interface as an open research problem.

• taste - finger feel for good developer experience.

• typist - you explain complex ideas clearly under deadline.

• human - you build trust and admit what you don't know.

OPTIONAL

• years of Python in production (Typescript a plus).

• API platform, iPaaS, or integration-heavy background.

• prior FDE / solutions-architect role selling to big tech.

• frontier output: MCP servers, tool-use evals, agent-tooling writeups.

source_jobs.title: Founding Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

Our largest customers are betting meaningful parts of their AI infrastructure on Composio. As our founding Customer Success Manager, you will own a book of high-value enterprise and growth accounts, build the relationships that turn customers into champions, and create the CS playbooks that scale with us.


WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - manage a book of Composio's largest enterprise and growth accounts as their primary point of contact and internal advocate

 - lead structured onboarding so customers reach value quickly with clean technical integration and clear success metrics

 - drive deep product adoption across teams, integrations, and workflows

 - own NRR for your book: identify expansion opportunities, lead renewal conversations, and partner with AEs on upsells and cross-sells

 - build executive relationships across champions, economic buyers, and technical decision-makers

 - serve as the voice of the customer internally by bringing product gaps, friction points, and patterns directly to product and engineering

 - run a structured QBR cadence with top accounts to review usage, align on roadmap, and surface strategic opportunities

 - build the CS playbook from scratch: onboarding frameworks, health scoring, escalation protocols, and renewal processes

 - partner with Support to ensure enterprise customers receive fast, high-quality resolution on technical issues


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - 4-7 years in Customer Success, Technical Account Management, or Solutions roles at a developer tools, infrastructure, or API-first SaaS company

 - proven track record owning NRR and renewals for enterprise accounts - you have grown a book, not just maintained one

 - technical fluency: you can understand APIs, SDKs, auth, integrations, agent workflows, and customer architecture well enough to be useful

 - excellent relationship management across technical champions, executives, and cross-functional internal teams

 - strong written communication and operating cadence: crisp recaps, useful QBRs, clean escalation notes, and no loose threads

 - builder mindset: you are excited to create the first CS systems instead of inheriting mature ones

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.


WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - define how the world talks about Composio - positioning, messaging, narrative, and proof across every surface

 - own product launches end to end: angle, sequence, channels, assets, internal alignment, and post-launch learning

 - identify and close content gaps - case studies, landing pages, comparison pages, white papers, docs, and sales enablement

 - build agentic workflows that make the whole marketing team faster

 - track the ecosystem and turn competitor/customer insight into sharper messaging

 - work closely with engineering so technical depth becomes clear customer-facing language


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - core product marketing
   
   - you have done product marketing at a developer tools or developer infrastructure company
   
   - you have built positioning and messaging for a technical product from scratch
   
   - you have owned at least one significant product launch end to end

 - technical fluency - you can sit in engineering discussions and translate what matters without needing everything spoon-fed

 - taste - you know when copy is sharp, when it is vague, and when the audience will not care

 - typist - you write strong launch copy, landing pages, blog posts, and internal narratives

 - human - you build trust and admit what you do not know


OPTIONAL

 - AI, agents, or developer infrastructure experience

 - experience building sales enablement or competitive content

 - built internal workflows using LLMs or agents

 - worked directly with founders or engineering leads on narrative

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Product Engineering Lead
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.




WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - own enterprise product end to end - pre-sales technical validation, POC support, weekly customer syncs, launch readiness, and post-launch iteration

 - turn ambiguous enterprise requests into product decisions, design docs, tradeoffs, and timelines engineering can defend

 - live close enough to the codebase to estimate complexity, spot bad architecture, and push back on customer requests that should not become product

 - work daily with revenue, engineering, and solutions to translate customer pain into the right roadmap priorities

 - build the enterprise playbook - escalation paths, quality bars, launch process, and repeatable ways to go from 5 enterprise customers to 50

 - protect the product from bespoke services work while still making strategic customers feel heard and unblocked


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - enterprise product judgment
   
   - you have owned product decisions under pressure and can explain the tradeoffs without hiding behind process
   
   - you have told a strategic customer no, or not yet, and kept trust in the room
   
   - you think in systems and primitives, not feature requests

 - technical depth
   
   - you can hold your own in architecture discussions with senior engineers
   
   - you can read enough code to understand what is actually hard
   
   - engineers trust your product judgment because it is grounded in reality

 - enterprise taste - you know when to slow down for reliability, security, and rollout quality

 - operator - you can create the missing process, run it yourself, and delete it when it stops helping

 - typist - you write clear docs, crisp customer notes, and product specs people actually read

 - human - you build trust and admit what you don't know


OPTIONAL

 - developer tools, infrastructure, or ai platform experience

 - started a company, sold something technical, or owned a meaningful P&L

 - experience taking enterprise products from founder-led motion to repeatable motion


HOW TO APPLY

Email hiring@composio.dev with your resume and a short note on a product decision you owned that a customer disagreed with.

source_jobs.title: Founding RevOps
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

Our go-to-market motion is working, but too much of it still runs on instinct and tribal knowledge. As Composio's first dedicated RevOps hire, you will build the revenue system underneath it: clean data, tight processes, the right tooling, and visibility for fast decisions.


WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - own CRM architecture, data model, hygiene standards, custom objects, automation, and integrations across the GTM stack

 - build pipeline reporting and forecasting dashboards that give leadership real-time visibility into what is coming and why

 - select, administer, and connect GTM tooling across sequencing, enrichment, intent data, routing, attribution, and AI-assisted outreach

 - design lead routing, scoring, and SLA frameworks so every inbound lead gets to the right rep at the right time with the right context

 - partner with sales leadership on territory design, quota setting, and compensation plan modeling

 - document and operationalize sales stages, exit criteria, and handoffs from first touch to close

 - build onboarding and enablement infrastructure that helps new GTM hires ramp faster

 - serve as the connective layer between Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Product on pipeline and revenue data


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - 3-6 years in RevOps, Sales Ops, or GTM Ops, ideally at a high-growth SaaS or developer-tools company at the Series A-C stage

 - deep CRM expertise: you have built a data model from scratch, not just maintained one someone handed you

 - strong analytical instincts: comfortable in SQL or BI tools, able to build a forecast model in a spreadsheet and a Salesforce report in the same afternoon

 - hands-on experience with modern GTM tooling such as Clay, Apollo, Gong, Salesforce, HubSpot, or equivalents

 - experience supporting the full customer lifecycle: marketing handoff, sales process, post-sale expansion, renewals, and reporting

 - ability to translate ambiguity into process without burying the team in process theater

 - high trust operator: clean data, clear docs, fast follow-through, and strong judgment around what actually matters

source_jobs.title: Founding Talent Architect
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.


WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - own sourcing across every open role - Github, X, hackathon leaderboards, paper authors, open source communities, and the places our customers live

 - run technical screens yourself: read code, ask useful follow-ups, and separate signal from noise

 - build the recruiting playbook we do not have yet - outreach, rubrics, calibration, scorecards, and weekly hiring reviews

 - own pipeline metrics and tell us where the system is broken: conversion, source quality, time to offer, and offer acceptance

 - partner with engineering and GTM leads to turn vague hiring needs into target profiles, sourcing plans, and screens we trust

 - dogfood Composio to automate outreach, candidate tracking, and reporting


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - technical recruiting
   
   - you can read code well enough to evaluate a take-home or Github profile without an engineer in the room
   
   - you have built a recruiting process from scratch, not just inherited one

 - operator - you can bring structure to ambiguous hiring needs and keep the loop moving without founder babysitting

 - ai native
   
   - you use LLMs in your daily workflow
   
   - you are comfortable writing small scripts or agents to automate repetitive work

 - typist - you can write clear outreach, rubrics, docs, and hiring notes

 - human - you build trust and admit what you do not know


OPTIONAL

 - founding recruiter or agency experience at a developer tools or infra company

 - experience hiring across both engineering and GTM

 - presence in developer communities through events, Discord, X, or open source

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer, Developer Relations
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

We're hiring our developer-facing voice: someone technical enough to build the demo, and funny enough that people actually stop scrolling to watch it. You'll own how the AI community meets Composio.



WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - Build in public. Ship demos, prototypes, and technical content that show what's actually possible with Composio - across whatever format fits: X threads, short-form video, YouTube, a livestream, a podcast appearance. If it shows what agents plus tools can do, you make it, and you narrate it as you go.

 - Own our voice. Run our presence on X and LinkedIn, and grow your own account as a technical voice people in the AI community actually follow.

 - Show up in the real world. Represent Composio at SF meetups, hackathons, and developer conferences. Build genuine relationships with the people building agents, and share your learnings with the world.

 - Bias towards action. Ship the narrow, signal-generating version today - the post, the demo, the two-minute video - read what lands, then either double down or kill it. Don't wait for permission to try something.

 - Work across the company. Partner with product, engineering, and marketing to turn what you're hearing from developers into shipped product and better positioning.
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

The hard-to-teach part is taste and timing: knowing what developers find genuinely cool versus what reads as marketing, and producing quality content developers actually want to engage with. Technical credibility is required - you build with our API and ship demos - but it's the foundation here, not what sets you apart. That part is the taste and the humor.

 - You can actually build. You've written real code, you can wire up an API and our SDK, and you can ship an interesting demo.

 - You genuinely like being public. Posting, filming, speaking, being on camera - this energizes you rather than drains you. You can make a technical thing legible and exciting to a scrolling developer.

 - Opinionated, with a high bar. You have strong views on what makes content good, and you hold yourself — and us — to them. You'd rather kill a mediocre post than ship it, and you'll push the whole company to be better too.

 - Comfortable across formats. You'd rather film a two-minute demo than write a press release. Text, video, audio, stage - you move between them without losing your voice.

 - AI-native. You build with LLMs and keep up with what's happening in the ecosystem.

 - Owner. You take a goal, decide what to make, ship it, measure whether it landed, and flex across all of the above - without waiting for a content calendar to tell you what to post.

 - Human. You build trust and admit what you don't know.
   
   


STRONG PREFERENCE FOR

 - Based in SF (or open to relocation)

 - An existing audience or a track record of building in public

 - Hands-on experience with agents, LLMs, MCP, and the modern AI dev stack
   


BONUS POINTS

 - A presence in the AI community through open source, writing, or building in public

source_jobs.title: Account Executive - Digital Natives
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

As Account Executive for Digital Natives, you will own new business with high-growth startups, developer-led companies, and fast-moving software teams building agents or embedding AI into their products. You will prospect, run sharp discovery, close deals, and help define the repeatable sales motion for this segment.


WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - own the full sales cycle across digital-native accounts: prospecting, qualification, discovery, technical validation, pricing, negotiation, close, and expansion

 - run consultative sales conversations with technical buyers and translate agent/tooling problems into a clear Composio value story

 - partner with Solutions Architecture to design pilots, unblock technical evaluations, and convert successful POCs into contracts

 - develop repeatable messaging, discovery notes, objection handling, competitive positioning, and deal patterns for the digital-native segment

 - work directly with product and engineering when customer pain points should become product primitives

 - meet and exceed individual quarterly and annual sales targets


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - 3-5+ years of full-cycle sales experience, ideally selling developer tools, infrastructure, AI products, APIs, or technical SaaS to high-growth companies

 - technical curiosity - you can understand APIs, SDKs, auth, integrations, agent workflows, and technical buying processes well enough to hold your own

 - strong written and verbal communication: tight follow-ups, clear framing, simple stories about complex products

 - high agency and urgency: you are comfortable when the playbook is still being written and can create structure without waiting for it

 - taste for great developer experience and the ability to spot when a customer is excited, confused, blocked, or not yet ready

 - human - you build trust, ask direct questions, admit what you do not know, and follow through

source_jobs.title: $1 engineer
source_jobs.description: https://x.com/victorgu0/status/2056858984511107451?s=20

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff, Enterprise Product
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), and Gokul Rajaram. We've 3x'd our ARR this year, and our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

Enterprise Product sits where the hardest customer workflows become durable product. You will work directly with enterprise customers and FDE, turn ambiguous asks into product primitives, and ship the code that makes the next customer easier than the last.

WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

1. Turn large enterprise APIs into agent-usable tool surfaces: tool granularity, composite tools, descriptions, scopes, auth, and evals.

2. Productize repeated FDE work into self-service patching, QA harnesses, deployment paths, admin controls, docs, and guardrails.

3. Make enterprise deployments repeatable across hosted, VPC, on-prem, regulated, and BYOK environments.

4. Build the product layer that lets Composio scale from bespoke enterprise engagements to repeatable enterprise motion.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

• Own enterprise product surfaces end to end: discovery, spec, design, implementation, QA, release, adoption, and iteration.

• Ship production code across toolkits, SDKs, auth and scope flows, admin surfaces, deployment tooling, and customer-facing enterprise features.

• Work with FDE, customers, and core engineering to convert field pain into crisp product requirements and shipped primitives.

• Build evals and QA loops that prove enterprise workflows are reliable, not just demo-ready.

• Write the specs, docs, launch notes, and customer-facing updates that make the work easy to adopt.

"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per se

• Product-minded engineer: you turn messy workflows into clean product, then ship the code.

• Enterprise integration depth: OAuth, consent, scopes, service accounts, webhooks, rate limits, and strange API behavior do not scare you.

• Customer ownership: you can take a named customer or product surface from first conversation to live usage.

• AI native: you have built with language models, built for language models, and have opinions on agents, MCP, evals, and tool choice.

• Systems judgment: you think in rollout paths, rollback paths, security exposure, version skew, and maintainability.

• Clear writer: your specs, docs, and customer updates earn trust under deadline.

OPTIONAL

• Python in production; TypeScript a plus.

• API platform, iPaaS, developer tools, infra, or integration-heavy background.

• Prior FDE, solutions architect, sales engineer, or enterprise product engineering role.

• Kubernetes, Helm, Temporal, Postgres, Replicated, cloud secret managers, or packaged self-hosted delivery.

• Security and compliance exposure: SOC 2, Vanta-style programs, questionnaires, CVE triage, or RCAs.

source_jobs.title: SDET
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COMPOSIO

At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x'd our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Build Automation Frameworks & Testing Infrastructure – Design, develop, and own automation frameworks, reusable test utilities, and quality tooling that enable scalable testing, validation, and release confidence across Composio's integrations, connectors, and agent workflows.

 - Validate Complex Integration Workflows – Design and execute test scenarios for critical integration workflows across internal and external systems, covering APIs, contracts, compatibility, authentication, webhooks, retries, idempotency, asynchronous workflows, and third-party dependencies. Identify edge cases and ensure reliable system behavior.

 - Enable Automation-Driven Quality Signoff – Build and maintain automated test suites, quality gates, and CI/CD checks that provide release confidence through functional, API, integration, contract, and regression testing. Analyse quality signals and engineering metrics to identify risks, prevent regressions, and support data-driven release decisions.

 - Build AI-Powered Quality Systems – Build AI-powered validation systems, agents, and automated workflows that help validate integrations, detect regressions, investigate failures, and scale quality across rapidly evolving products.

 - Improve Testing Strategy & Quality Practices – Contribute to testing approaches across integration, API, contract, and end-to-end layers. Improve test coverage, establish effective testing patterns, and strengthen validation practices across the engineering lifecycle.

 - Improve Reliability of AI-Native Products – Develop testing and evaluation approaches for AI-powered workflows, tool-calling agents, and other systems where outputs may vary between executions. Help measure quality, identify gaps, and improve confidence in AI-driven experiences.

 - Improve Production Quality & Reliability – Investigate production issues, contribute to root-cause analysis, enhance test observability, and implement preventive improvements that increase system reliability and platform stability.

 - Raise the Engineering Quality Bar – Partner with engineers to improve testability, provide input on design decisions, apply quality best practices, and help teams build reliable, testable systems.
   


MUST HAVES


AUTOMATION & QUALITY ENGINEERING

 - You build automation frameworks, quality platforms, and developer tooling that engineers rely on.

 - You write clean, maintainable automation and validation code, and understand how to apply the right quality strategy across API, integration, contract, and end-to-end layers.

 - You care about quality outcomes, CI/CD quality gates, release confidence, and reducing flaky tests—not just test execution.


COMPLEX SYSTEMS & INTEGRATIONS

 - You can reason about API behavior, authentication, retries, idempotency, eventual consistency, and distributed systems.

 - You have experience testing, validating, and debugging integration-heavy systems involving multiple services and external dependencies.

 - You use logs, traces, metrics, and production signals to investigate failures and improve system reliability.


AI-NATIVE

 - You actively use AI to accelerate testing, debugging, automation, or engineering workflows—with validation and critical thinking, not blind trust.

 - You

source_jobs.title: Growth and Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

Our product is used by prosumers (MCP / CLI), developers (SDK), and enterprises. The job of the growth team is to increase usage and revenue across all three products. We accomplish this with a ruthless focus on analytics to inform the products we ship, features we highlight, and leads we hand off to sales. We're looking to hire a talented engineer who is as comfortable in SQL as they are in nextJS - someone who's excited to own the analytics foundations of the company and use it to drive our next phase of growth.




WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - Own our data foundation. Build and run the data lake and analytics stack that ties together all three products - MCP/CLI, SDK, and enterprise. You'll own Snowflake end to end: building pipelines, modeling clean generated tables, keeping the data trustworthy, and being the person who can answer the questions nobody else can.

 - Turn behavior into decisions. Dig into how people actually use Composio, chase down the trickiest analytics questions, and translate what you find into roadmap calls - what to double down on, what to kill, and what deserves the next bet. This work directly guides where product invests.

 - Build the product-led sales engine. Instrument our funnels and flag PLS signals into Snowflake so consumer and developer usage becomes a clean, agile handoff to sales. Connecting a prosumer's MCP usage or a developer's SDK adoption to the right enterprise conversation is core to how we grow.

 - Ship growth features in the product. You're as comfortable in Next.js as in SQL. You'll build in-product upsells, sharpen onboarding and ship new onboarding flows, and drive in-app actions like booking a sales meeting - without ever making it feel bolted on. The experience stays delightful.

 - Own activation and retention. Run email activation and reactivation campaigns, close funnel gaps, and take these problems from goal to shipped without waiting for a PM to turn them into tickets.

 - Scope ruthlessly. Ship the narrow, signal-generating version today, read the data, then either kill the idea or build it properly.

 - Work across the company. Partner with product, sales, marketing, and engineering to turn distribution ideas into shipped product.
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

The data instinct is the part that's hard to teach. Strong engineers can round out the rest quickly, so we weight it accordingly.

 - Data fluency (non-negotiable). You write SQL fluently, are at home living in a warehouse like Snowflake, and can model data, stand up tracking, build dashboards, and reason clearly from messy funnel data. You're genuinely curious about user behavior and driven to answer the hardest questions. This is the heart of the role.

 - Strong engineer. You ship polished frontend and backend systems and context-switch across codebases without losing speed. If your product-engineering chops are still catching up to your data ability, that's fine - we'd rather hire a great data mind who's hungry to round out the full stack than the reverse.

 - Growth taste. You know the difference between a cheap experiment and a sloppy product, and you'd rather ship a narrow signal-generating version today than a perfect unknown next week.

 - AI-native. You build with LLMs and keep up with what's happening in the ecosystem.

 - O

As Enterprise Account Executive, you will build and close new business with larger customers betting on agents and AI infrastructure. You will own enterprise opportunities from first conversation through procurement, security review, technical validation, close, and expansion. Location: San Francisco, New York, Austin, or remote.


WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - own the full sales cycle across enterprise accounts: prospecting, qualification, discovery, technical validation, pricing, negotiation, close, and expansion

 - run consultative conversations with engineering, product, AI, security, and procurement stakeholders

 - partner with Solutions Architecture to shape pilots, unblock evaluations, and turn technical success into signed contracts

 - multi-thread accounts and build champions across technical users, economic buyers, and executives

 - develop repeatable enterprise messaging, discovery notes, objection handling, competitive positioning, and deal patterns

 - bring product and engineering into customer pain points that should become platform primitives

 - meet and exceed individual quarterly and annual sales targets


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - 5+ years of full-cycle sales experience, ideally selling technical products to engineering, IT, AI, or platform teams

 - a track record closing complex enterprise deals with multiple stakeholders, security/procurement/legal review, and technical evaluation

 - technical curiosity - you can understand APIs, SDKs, auth, integrations, agent workflows, and technical buying processes well enough to hold your own

 - strong written and verbal communication: crisp follow-ups, clear deal framing, and simple stories about complex products

 - high agency and urgency: you are comfortable when the playbook is still being written and can create structure without waiting for it

 - human - you build trust, ask direct questions, admit what you do not know, and follow through

source_jobs.title: Founding BDR Leader
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

We are at an inflection point: strong inbound demand, a product that is winning, and a sales motion that is just beginning to be formalized. This is a founding role for someone who can build the BDR function from scratch as a hands-on operator, not just a manager.


WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - own the BDR function end-to-end: strategy, process, tooling, hiring, and day-to-day execution

 - carry a personal pipeline quota in addition to team targets - lead from the front, not the whiteboard

 - build and iterate the outbound playbook: ICP definition, sequencing, messaging, channel mix, and account prioritization

 - hire, onboard, and develop BDRs; create the hiring bar, ramp program, and performance framework from scratch

 - partner with Marketing to turn content, events, and intent signals into pipeline quickly

 - work with Account Executives to define qualified opportunities and keep handoffs tight

 - own the BDR tech stack across CRM hygiene, enrichment, sequencing, AI-assisted outreach, and reporting

 - report pipeline, activity, and conversion metrics weekly and surface insights that shape product and GTM strategy


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - 3-6 years in BDR or SDR roles, with at least 1-2 years in a lead or player-coach capacity at a high-growth SaaS or developer-tools company

 - proven track record building outbound playbooks from scratch, not just executing someone else's

 - strong hiring instincts: you know how to spot coachable, hungry talent and develop it into top performers

 - technical fluency: comfortable talking to developers, understanding APIs and infrastructure products, and demoing complex tools at a high level

 - deep familiarity with modern GTM tools such as Clay, Apollo, Gong, Salesforce, AI-assisted outreach, and enrichment tooling

 - excitement about using AI and automation to build a leaner, higher-output team

 - bias toward action and experimentation: you would rather ship a good playbook today and iterate than wait for perfect conditions

Apply with the role you want, what makes you exceptional, and the first thing you would build or fix at Composio.

source_jobs.title: Deployed Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools. We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), and Gokul Rajaram. We've 3x'd our ARR this year, and our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

running stateful agent infrastructure inside someone else's cluster is new territory. this role defines what reliable, repeatable on-prem agent deployment looks like, across the customers running us in their own environments.

THE FRONTIER YOU'LL WORK ON

• fleet-managing agent runtimes across air-gapped, regulated, BYOK environments has no playbook. you define one.

• turning hand-managed on-prem deployments into a repeatable product. the difference between 5 self-hosted customers and 50.

• every incident, CVE, and upgrade wave here is unsolved at the ecosystem level. your runbooks lead the industry.

WHAT YOU'LL DO?

• own the on-prem lifecycle: helm charts, upgrades, the weekly release train, rollback paths.

• drive chart convergence: diverged customer forks back onto one supported chart, with guardrails.

• build structured onboarding (replicated or equivalent) so a new customer deploys in days, not weeks.

• debug across the stack: temporal workflows, k8s init ordering, storage backends, auth-refresh pipelines.

• own the security surface: CVE triage, patching, RCAs, bulletins. keep customers running when releases freeze.

• co-own per-customer tool patching with the platform team.

• dogfood Composio on your own deployment and support workflows.

"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

• core infra engineering

– you've run software in environments you don't control: on-prem, VPC, air-gapped.

– deep kubernetes and helm: chart authoring, init-job ordering, multi-environment releases.

– you keep systems reliable while the product moves fast.

• fleet judgment

– you think in version skew, upgrade waves, and rollback paths across many instances.

– you separate one customer's bespoke ask from the primitive every install needs.

• ai native

– you have built with the language models.

– you have opinions on agents, MCP, and where they break in real deployments.

• linux - comfortable deep in the stack.

• typist - your RCAs and security notes earn customer trust.

• human - you build trust and admit what you don't know.

OPTIONAL

• temporal, Postgres, cloud secret managers (GCP / AWS).

• replicated or packaged self-hosted delivery (kots, airgap bundles).

• compliance: SOC 2, Vanta-style programs, security questionnaires.

• major open source contributions.

source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.



We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customer range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.







WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - evolve our platform primitives and APIs — auth, automatic refreshes, triggers, tool search, tool planning, orchestrating and managing sandboxes

 - manage multiple runtimes for code execution across lambdas and firecracker

 - performance optimization — tracing, CPU/heap profiling, optimizing db queries, optimizing temporal workflows.

 - work closely with product engineering teams and customers to win their workloads, improving the product in the process

 - writing articulate docs




"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - core platform engineering
   
   - you've worked extensively in scaling backend distributed systems
   
   - you know how to maintain reliable systems while shipping fast
   
   - you are able to hold many different parts of the platform in mind at once

 - ai native
   
   - you have built with the language models
   
   - you have built for the language models

 - linux — you are comfortable in a linux enviornment

 - typist — you can write docs well and explain complex ideas clearly

 - human — you build trust and admit what you don’t know




OPTIONAL

 - multiple years of experience writing Typescript, Go

 - contributions to a major open source project

 - started companies or build large side projects

source_jobs.title: Product Intern
source_jobs.description:
WHO ARE WE?

At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customer range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.



WHAT WILL YOU DO?

Building Agents

 - Write down the manual playbook as you run it, then work out what a person should never have to touch again

 - Build AI agents that watch inboxes, send the follow-ups, and keep the tracker current on their own

 - Turn repetitive outreach and tracking into workflows that keep running without you in the loop

 - Multiply yourself, so one person can keep hundreds of apps moving at once without anything slipping
   
   Operations

 - Shepherd new app connectors from first request to live: securing OAuth approvals and API access, spinning up sandbox credentials and test accounts, and clearing whatever verification or paperwork stands in the way

 - Hold hundreds of apps in view at once, each parked at a different stage of approval

 - Chase approvals until they actually land, not until the first email goes unanswered

 - Make the demo videos and scope-mapping docs that move partners from maybe to yes

 - Own the status of every app, so engineering, product, and partners never have to ask where things stand
   
   
   


WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR?

 - You already build with AI every day. It's part of how you work, not a box you'd tick out of curiosity

 - You stay with a problem until it's actually solved, sending the fifth follow-up long after most people quit at the first

 - You can keep fifty-plus threads in motion at once without letting one slip through

 - You work well without a map: you find the real problem before anyone hands it to you, then go fix it

 - Your first instinct is to automate a task rather than do it a second time

 - Automation, agents, workflows, and operations are the tools you use to hit a goal, not the job itself. We want someone who cares about achieving the goal, not a "whatever it takes" task executor
   

On the practical side:

 - An engineering background — BTech, CS, or similar — ideally with a few projects you've actually built and shipped

 - Writing clean enough to land in a partner's or customer's inbox without a second draft

 - Able to be in Bengaluru, in person, for the full 2 months (we are willing to extend the internship tenure to 6 months if more projects come in)

 - Final-year students and recent grads equally welcome, including if you're holding a full-time offer that starts later in the year

source_jobs.title: Founding BDR
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.

As a founding BDR, you will turn high inbound interest and targeted outbound demand into qualified opportunities. You will create excellent first-touch experiences for teams exploring agentic AI and learn how to use AI, automation, and Composio itself to become a next-generation GTM operator.


WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - follow up with inbound and outbound prospects across phone, email, LinkedIn, communities, and events

 - uncover fit, urgency, pain, and potential value quickly enough to create useful meetings for Account Executives

 - build targeted outbound lists and research accounts deeply enough to write specific, relevant outreach

 - partner with Account Executives to hand off qualified meetings and keep the buyer experience clean from first touch through discovery

 - use Composio and modern sales tools to automate list building, enrichment, and sequencing so your time goes into conversations

 - capture field insights that sharpen our sales plays, messaging, and product roadmap

 - experiment with new channels, triggers, and sequences; treat outreach as a craft and improve it continuously


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - 1-2 years in a Sales, SDR, or BDR role in SaaS or tech, or exceptional drive and curiosity if you are earlier in your career

 - clear, confident communication with both developers and business practitioners

 - genuine interest in AI tools and modern GTM - you have already started experimenting, not just reading about it

 - comfort with ambiguity: you can follow a repeatable process while improving it at the same time

 - persistence, organization, and positive energy: you follow up, follow through, and bring quality to every interaction

source_jobs.title: internet gremlin
source_jobs.description:
At Composio, we are building infrastructure that allows agents to communicate with the tools you use for work including Github, Gmail, Notion, Salesforce, etc. We are a small team of engineers wrangling problems from context to search, that help us provide the most capable bridge between your agents and your tools.

We raised a $25M Series A from Lightspeed https://lsvp.com/stories/investing-in-composio-building-the-backbone-of-ai-agent-intelligence/ with some incredible angels like Guillermo Rauch (CEO of Vercel), Dharmesh Shah (CTO of Hubspot), Gokul Rajaram. Beginning of this year we 3x our ARR, our customers range from your friends in the YC batch to Wabi, Glean, Zoom and many more.


WHAT YOU'LL DO?

 - run Composio on the internet without making us sound like a generic B2B SaaS account

 - turn launches, bugs, demos, customer stories, and founder chaos into posts people actually want to read

 - live on X, LinkedIn, Reddit, Discord, and wherever agent builders are arguing this week

 - work with product and engineering to find the weird technical details that make good distribution

 - measure what works, kill what does not, and build repeatable social/content loops

 - roast our current socials responsibly and then make them better


"MUST HAVES"

if you are very good, nothing is a must per-se

 - internet native
   
   - you understand reply culture, dev twitter, AI discourse, and why most brand accounts are unbearable
   
   - you can be funny without being cringe

 - taste - you know the difference between a sharp post, a try-hard post, and a post that should stay in drafts

 - technical curiosity - you can understand enough of the product to make the content true

 - operator - you can ship every day without needing a six-week content calendar

 - human - you build trust and admit what you do not know


OPTIONAL

 - has grown an account, community, newsletter, or weird internet project

 - has built with AI tools or agents

 - has survived a community note and learned something useful from it


HOW TO APPLY

DM @karanvaidya6, @cryogenicplanet, @itsaqd, or @composio with a roast of our current socials and one example of what you would post instead.

notes: Highly technical job postings, with a focus on engineering and product development. No explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance requirements.
